display.purpose: Onboarding, payment review, and screens that need one dominant statement.

title.purpose: Modal, card, and federation summary titles.

label.purpose: Metadata, small labels, and status descriptors.

mono.purpose: Recovery words, ids, invoices, and exact technical strings.

shadows.name: primary-soft
shadows.purpose: Primary-soft buttons, FABs, and slider handles.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Button, input, tab, and panel state feedback.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: High-commitment action button for payment, setup, and confirmation flows.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { min-height: var(--vipr-control-height-lg, 54px); padding: 0 20px; border: 0; border-radius: var(--vipr-radius-button-lg, 18px); background: var(--vipr-gradient-primary, linear-gradient(135deg, rgba(162,43,255,1), rgba(116,0,255,.96))); color: var(--vipr-text-primary, #fff); box-shadow: var(--vipr-shadow-primary, 0 12px 28px rgba(128,0,255,.24)); font: 600 var(--vipr-font-size-body, 1rem)/1.2 Roboto, system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0; transition: transform .2s ease, box-shadow .2s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(156,39,255,.55); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Quiet alternative action that stays available without competing with the primary action.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { min-height: var(--vipr-control-height-md, 48px); padding: 0 20px; border: 1px solid var(--vipr-color-surface-border, rgba(255,255,255,.08)); border-radius: var(--vipr-radius-control, 14px); background: var(--vipr-color-surface-soft, rgba(255,255,255,.04)); color: var(--vipr-text-secondary, rgba(255,255,255,.78)); font: 600 var(--vipr-font-size-body, 1rem)/1.2 Roboto, system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0; transition: background-color .18s ease, border-color .18s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: var(--vipr-color-input-bg-hover, rgba(255,255,255,.065)); border-color: var(--vipr-color-input-border-hover, rgba(255,255,255,.08)); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(156,39,255,.55); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Strong Surface Card
components.description: Raised but quiet grouping surface for payment flows, summaries, and backup panels.
components.html:
Selected federation

Review the federation before moving funds.
components.css: .ds-card-strong { padding: var(--vipr-space-5, 20px); border: 1px solid var(--vipr-color-surface-raised-border, rgba(255,255,255,.075)); border-radius: var(--vipr-radius-card, 24px); background: var(--vipr-surface-card-bg-strong, rgba(15,16,22,.92)); color: var(--vipr-text-primary, #fff); box-shadow: var(--vipr-surface-card-shadow, inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,.035)); } .ds-card-strong h3 { margin: 0; font: 700 var(--vipr-font-size-section-title, 1.05rem)/1.25 Roboto, system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0; } .ds-card-strong p { margin: 8px 0 0; color: var(--vipr-text-muted, rgba(255,255,255,.62)); font: 400 var(--vipr-font-size-caption, .9rem)/1.45 Roboto, system-ui, sans-serif; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Dark form field for invoices, memos, addresses, relay URLs, and recovery input.
components.css: .ds-input { display: grid; gap: 6px; color: var(--vipr-text-muted, rgba(255,255,255,.62)); font: 500 var(--vipr-font-size-label, .78rem)/1.2 Roboto, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-input input { min-height: var(--vipr-control-height-md, 48px); padding: 0 16px; border: 1px solid var(--vipr-color-input-border, rgba(255,255,255,.055)); border-radius: var(--vipr-radius-input, 16px); background: var(--vipr-color-input-bg, rgba(255,255,255,.05)); color: var(--vipr-text-primary, #fff); font: 400 var(--vipr-font-size-body, 1rem)/1.45 Roboto, system-ui, sans-serif; box-shadow: var(--vipr-control-panel-inset-shadow, inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,.018)); transition: background-color .18s ease, border-color .18s ease, box-shadow .18s ease; } .ds-input input:hover { background: var(--vipr-color-input-bg-hover, rgba(255,255,255,.065)); border-color: var(--vipr-color-input-border-hover, rgba(255,255,255,.08)); } .ds-input input:focus { outline: none; border-color: var(--vipr-color-input-border-focus, rgba(156,39,255,.5)); box-shadow: var(--vipr-control-panel-focus-shadow, 0 0 0 1px rgba(156,39,255,.12)); }

components.name: Chip
components.description: Compact context badge for selected federation, module, status, and metadata.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; min-height: 24px; padding: 0 10px; border-radius: var(--vipr-radius-pill, 999px); background: var(--vipr-chip-bg, rgba(255,255,255,.075)); color: var(--vipr-text-secondary, rgba(255,255,255,.78)); font: 500 .78rem/1 Roboto, system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0; } .ds-chip:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(156,39,255,.55); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Bottom Navigation Tab
components.description: Quiet app-shell tab for Home, Federations, and Settings.
components.css: .ds-bottom-nav { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; gap: 6px; width: min(100%, 700px); padding: var(--vipr-space-2, 8px) var(--vipr-space-3, 12px) 6px; border-top: 1px solid var(--vipr-layout-footer-border, rgba(255,255,255,.012)); background: var(--vipr-layout-footer-bg, rgba(15,16,22,.96)); box-shadow: var(--vipr-layout-footer-shadow, 0 -6px 18px rgba(0,0,0,.1)); } .ds-bottom-nav button { flex: 1 1 0; min-height: 56px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: var(--vipr-radius-control, 14px); background: transparent; color: var(--vipr-text-muted, rgba(255,255,255,.62)); font: 500 .8rem/1 Roboto, system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0; transition: color 160ms ease, opacity 160ms ease; } .ds-bottom-nav button:hover { color: var(--vipr-text-primary, #fff); } .ds-bottom-nav button.is-active { color: var(--vipr-layout-tab-active, #a970ff); font-weight: 600; } .ds-bottom-nav button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(156,39,255,.55); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: QR Surface
components.description: High-contrast QR backing surface inside the otherwise dark app.
components.css: .ds-qr-surface { display: grid; place-items: center; width: 160px; aspect-ratio: 1; margin: 0; padding: var(--vipr-qr-padding, 10px); border-radius: var(--vipr-radius-md, 16px); background: var(--vipr-qr-surface-bg, #fff); box-shadow: var(--vipr-qr-surface-shadow, 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,.04)); } .ds-qr-surface div { width: 100%; height: 100%; border-radius: 8px; background: repeating-linear-gradient(90deg, #111 0 8px, transparent 8px 16px), repeating-linear-gradient(0deg, #111 0 8px, transparent 8px 16px); opacity: .9; }

narrative.overview:
Vipr's interface is a compact private wallet surface: dark, restrained, touch-first, and deliberately calm around moments where users move money or make trust decisions. It should feel like a secure working tool rather than a crypto spectacle. The visual system earns confidence through clear hierarchy, generous touch targets, predictable flow panels, and reusable vipr-* classes.

The physical scene is a user checking or sending funds on a phone in mixed ambient light, often with limited patience and real risk attached. Dark mode is justified by repeated mobile use, QR scanning, payment review, and a need to keep the interface quiet while amounts, federation names, invoices, recovery words, and warnings remain readable.

The system explicitly rejects the PRODUCT.md anti-references: a generic crypto trading app, exchange dashboard, neon-on-black Web3 landing page, speculative finance product, enterprise banking portal, corporate blue trust-washing, dense legalese, and sterile compliance-first layout.
keyCharacteristics: Compact mobile shells with a 700px app width ceiling and 16px edge insets.
Wider detail and backup panels use the documented wide widths (`760px`, `720px`) without changing the mobile-first rhythm.
Restrained dark neutrals with a rare violet accent for primary action and selection.
Tonal layering, borders, and inset highlights before heavy drop shadows.
Direct, plain-spoken copy that surfaces trust and custody risk at the relevant step.
Shared vipr-* classes and CSS tokens as the source of truth for new UI.

rules.name: The One Violet Rule
rules.body: Vault Violet is the only brand accent. Do not add extra brand hues for visual variety.

rules.name: The Signal Is Functional Rule
rules.body: Green, amber, red, and blue are status colors only. They must explain state, risk, or completion.

rules.name: The Rail Color Rule
rules.body: Bitcoin Gold and Lightning Gold identify payment rails. They are not general accent colors.

rules.name: The No Crypto Glow Rule
rules.body: Glows and radial accents must stay subtle and contextual. If the surface reads as neon-on-black Web3, it is wrong.

rules.name: The Amount First Rule
rules.body: In money movement screens, the amount and final action outrank decorative titles.

rules.name: The Zero Tracking Rule
rules.body: Default letter spacing is 0. Do not widen labels unless using the established eyebrow or recovery-word pattern.

rules.name: The Tonal First Rule
rules.body: Add depth with surface tint, border, and inset highlights before drop shadows.

rules.name: The Heavy Shadow Budget Rule
rules.body: Heavy shadows belong to overlays and page panels, not ordinary list items.

dos: Do use shared vipr-* classes and CSS tokens before creating local styles.
Do keep primary actions visibly tied to Vault Violet and keep secondary actions visually quieter.
Do surface selected federation, amount, destination, fees, and final action before payment commitment.
Do use Signal Amber for custody, backup, or federation-risk warnings.
Do use Bitcoin Gold and Lightning Gold only for payment-rail identity.
Do respect WCAG AA, reduced motion preferences, keyboard access, and non-color status cues.
Do keep mobile edge insets aligned to --vipr-space-4 unless scanner, modal, or QR surfaces intentionally differ.
Do keep QR surfaces high-contrast even when they require a white backing surface inside the dark app.
donts: Don't make Vipr look like a generic crypto trading app, exchange dashboard, neon-on-black Web3 landing page, or speculative finance product.
Don't use excessive glow effects, cyberpunk palettes, token-price visual language, decorative gradients, or visual drama that competes with payment comprehension.
Don't make Vipr look like an enterprise banking portal with corporate blue trust-washing, dense legalese, or sterile compliance-first layout.
Don't hardcode colors, radii, spacing, shadows, or typography in components when a vipr-* token already exists.
Don't use color alone to communicate status, risk, success, or failure.
Don't reuse scanner, QR, wizard, or backup special-purpose tokens as generic decoration on normal wallet screens.
Don't use side-stripe borders, gradient text, decorative glassmorphism, repeated identical card grids, or modal-first solutions unless the interaction truly requires a modal.
